districts.label: Central Bundle Cache
districts.blurb: Hub-local scratch for centrally-stored bundles and view-server bookkeeping (per-repo-slug subfolders, active symlink, view-server pid/log) — currently holds test-run bundles from prior sessions committed to git rather than gitignored.

districts.label: Slash Commands
districts.blurb: Thin dispatchers (baseline.md, generate.md, view.md) that forward arguments straight into the odyssey skill — no logic of their own.

districts.label: Generation Scripts
districts.blurb: PEP 723 uv scripts doing the mechanical data movement the skill orchestrates: extract_story, extract_diffs, generate_prompts, generate_audio, verify_bundle.

districts.label: Odyssey Skill
districts.blurb: SKILL.md orchestration procedure plus on-demand reference docs (story-mode, decision-records-lite, baseline-derivation, adr-template, per-stack detection cards) that hold all the judgment-shaped guidance.

timeline.title: docs: add CLAUDE.md with codebase orientation + artifact feasibility findings
timeline.tagline: This PR proposes a single orientation doc so future sessions stop re-deriving the same layout and feasibility findings from scratch.

landscape.narration: This PR adds one new file, CLAUDE.md, 141 lines, at the repo root — no code changes.
landscape.voice: This PR is a documentation-only change. It adds one new file, CLAUDE dot M D, at the root of the repo — one hundred forty-one lines, no code touched.

problem_solution.problem: prodyssey has no in-repo orientation document. A session starting cold has to reconstruct the bundle output shape, the `<target>/.odyssey/` write boundary, and the viewer's sibling-file/relative-path dependencies by reading `SKILL.md`, the scripts, and `viewer/index.html` directly every time — including re-deriving conclusions the project has already reached once, like whether `viewer/index.html` can ship as a Claude Artifact.
problem_solution.solution: This PR proposes `CLAUDE.md`: an orientation doc covering layout, the generation flow (prereq gate → baseline → per-PR sweep → verify), the bundle output shape, and — grounded in an actual throwaway export built and published this session — the finding that the viewer is not artifact-safe as-is (sibling `<script src>` data loading, relative asset/audio paths, two external CDN calls) but renders correctly once flattened, with the 16 MiB cap and audio's ~33% base64 inflation named as the real constraint on a future export mode.
problem_solution.narration: Right now, understanding how this plugin's pieces fit together means reading the skill file and every script from scratch. This PR proposes writing that understanding down once — including a real experiment that already answered whether the bundle viewer could be published directly as a Claude Artifact.

beats.text: prodyssey's only structure-documenting file is `skills/odyssey/SKILL.md`, which describes orchestration procedure, not repo layout or bundle-shape conventions a session needs before touching code.

The viewer (`viewer/index.html`) depends on three things not visible from reading it alone: sibling `<script src="../data/*.js">` tags populating `window.STORY`/`ODYSSEY`/`DIFFS`/`ADRS`, relative asset/audio paths, and two external CDN requests (Google Fonts, jsdelivr Motion) — none of which are obvious without having built and served a real bundle.

Concretely: this session built a throwaway artifact-safe export earlier — literal JSON inlined for `window.STORY`/`ODYSSEY`/`DIFFS`/`ADRS`, asset paths rewritten to a `window.ODYSSEY_ASSETS` data-URI map, both CDN tags dropped — and it rendered correctly, PR nav/levels/hero image/audio dialog all worked. Without `CLAUDE.md`, the next session asking the same artifact-feasibility question would rebuild that same experiment instead of reading one paragraph.

problem_solution.voice: Before this PR, understanding how prodyssey's pieces fit together meant reading the orchestration skill and every script from scratch each time. This proposes writing that down once — including a real experiment, already run this session, that answered whether the bundle viewer could be published directly as a Claude Artifact.

architecture.narration: This PR touches no module boundary, dependency direction, or public interface — it is a documentation-only change with zero architecture decisions to record.
architecture.voice: This is a documentation-only change. It doesn't move a module boundary or a public interface, so there's no architecture decision to record here.

beats.text: Null decision: `CLAUDE.md` is prose only, added at the repo root. It doesn't move code between districts, add a dependency, or change any script's or the viewer's behavior — it only documents behavior and constraints (the artifact-feasibility finding, the bundle output shape) that already existed before this PR.

file_changes.narration: One file added: CLAUDE.md, at the repo root.

groups.title: Orientation doc
groups.note: New file, no existing file touched — codebase layout, generation flow, bundle output shape, and the artifact-feasibility experiment writeup, so a future session doesn't reconstruct any of it from scratch.
